text:
Once upon a time in a village surrounded by majestic forests full of woodland creatures, there lived a young hare named Benny Hop-a-long-the-head. Benny loved nothing more than exploring the woods and listening to the songs of the birds.

One day, Benny accidentally crossed paths with a tiny, brave frog named Flop. Flop loved to watch the birds sing and often dreamed of singing himself someday.

After a series of unlikely adventures, including a hilarious moment chasing away a pesky crow with his acrobatic hops, Benny and Flop became fast friends.

Together, they joined a group of friends who loved weaving wonderful bird songs into their morning forest songs. Whenever they sang, the feathered visitors would gather in delight.

One morning, as the sun painted the sky in shades of orange and purple, Benny sang a whimsical tune about a singing hummingbird. The birds joined in, each feather rustling a different melody. Birds far and wide came to listen, forming a incredible chorus.
